name: Classic Toys
description: Lego, Barbie, Hot Wheels, Tamagotchi, Magic 8-Ball — what's in the toybox.

questions.q: What number appears on the Magic 8-Ball?
Which country invented Lego in 1932?

source.notes: Founder Ole Kirk Christiansen. Name from Danish 'leg godt' (play well).

questions.q: What 1959 doll launched Mattel's empire?

source.notes: Created by Ruth Handler. First sold March 9, 1959.

questions.q: What's Barbie's full name?

source.notes: Named after Ruth Handler's daughter Barbara.

questions.q: What's Barbie's boyfriend's full name?

source.notes: Introduced 1961. Named after Ruth Handler's son Kenneth.

questions.q: Which company makes Hot Wheels?
What digital pet craze, launched 1996, had over 76 million sold by 2010?

source.notes: Made by Bandai. 'Tama' means 'egg' + 'tomodachi' (friend) in Japanese.

questions.q: What Bandai virtual-pet device, spun off into a 1997 anime, became a 1997-launched rival to Tamagotchi?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: replaced incorrect 'Tickle Me Elmo' answer (Tyco, plush, not Bandai). Digimon ('Digital Monsters') launched as a Bandai virtual-pet device in 1997 (boys' counterpart to Tamagotchi) and spun off into the popular 1999 anime.

questions.q: Which 1981 American Greetings plush toy line — colorful bears with belly-badge symbols — became a hit cartoon series?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: replaced meta 'all accepted' answer with single Care Bears answer plus real distractors. Care Bears were created by American Greetings in 1981 (with Kenner producing plush) and spawned the 1985-88 animated series.

questions.q: Which 1981 Hasbro creator (Bonnie Zacherle) launched a line of pastel toy ponies?
Which Mattel-owned doll line went to legal war with MGA Entertainment over the Bratz Dolls in 2008?

source.notes: Mattel sued because Bratz designer Carter Bryant created the dolls while at Mattel. Decade-long lawsuit.

questions.q: Which Hasbro toy launched in 1964 was the first to be called 'America's Movable Fighting Man'?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: removed meta 'both' option. G.I. Joe launched by Hasbro in 1964 with the 'America's Movable Fighting Man' slogan; Action Man was Palitoy's UK licensed rebrand starting 1966.

questions.q: What Pixar-themed Mattel diecast became huge in the 2000s?
Which Rubik's Cube creator was a Hungarian sculptor and architecture professor?

source.notes: Invented 1974, debuted globally as 'Rubik's Cube' in 1980.

questions.q: How many possible Rubik's Cube combinations are there?
Which classic 'flying disc' toy was popularized by Wham-O in 1957?

source.notes: Named after Frisbie Pie Company (whose tins college kids tossed).

questions.q: What 1983-launched cloth-and-vinyl Coleco doll line came with adoption certificates?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: corrected stem — Cabbage Patch Kids are cloth/vinyl (not plastic-coated), originated by Xavier Roberts ('Little People') in 1978 and mass-produced by Coleco starting 1983, peaking 1983-84 (not 1985).

questions.q: What 1996 small-business success story trading-card phenomenon began as 'Pocket Monsters' in Japan?

source.notes: Trading card game launched 1996 in Japan, 1999 in US.

questions.q: What 1990s-era circular cardboard disc collectible — originally based on Hawaiian juice-bottle caps — became a schoolyard craze?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: removed meta 'both accepted' option and fixed material descriptor — Pogs are cardboard (not plastic). 'POG' = Passion fruit-Orange-Guava juice in Hawaii; the bottle caps inspired the game.

questions.q: In what year did Mattel introduce Hot Wheels, its iconic die-cast toy car line?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: replaced fabricated '1959 Mattel plastic-interlocking-cars toy' premise (no such product). Hot Wheels launched May 1968 as Mattel's challenge to Matchbox.
